fields.description:
# Code-review 'new-behavior-without-test' landmine detector

## Context (discovered from grusome-uncheerful-nematode)
Child #7 originally bundled two changes: (a) the docs/llm-prompts nit down-rank (shipped in #7)
and (b) a grounding-assisted detector that fires when a diff adds new public behavior with no
correlated test. Investigation during the epic established that (b) needs a BESPOKE seam that
does not exist yet, so it was split out here to keep #7 landable.

## Why it needs a bespoke seam (not an existing one)
- Code-review DET detectors are **Engine B** grounding rules (gitleaks/opengrep-style YAML
  pattern rules) selected by `exec: DET` routing `detector` selectors and run via
  `run_detectors` -> `engine_b.scan`. A 'new-behavior-without-test' check requires diff-parsing
  the added public definitions AND cross-file correlation to test files — that is NOT the
  pattern-matching model Engine B implements.
- `rebar.grounding.resolve.extract_references_from_diff` extracts **imports only** (Python), not
  added symbol DEFINITIONS, so it cannot supply the added-definition set the detector needs.

## Sketch of the required seam
A Python code-review detector (in `code_review/detectors.py` or a new module) invoked in the DET
phase that: (1) parses the unified diff for newly-added exported/public defs via ast-grep /
universal-ctags (language-aware); (2) correlates against added/updated test files; (3) FIRES
(advisory/permissive first) when a new public def has no correlated test; (4) ABSTAINS
fail-open on: unparseable/garbled diff, no grounding backend available, or docs/config-only
change. Coaching surfaces through the existing `code_review_coach` path.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] A grounding-assisted `new_behavior_without_test` detector exists and is wired into the
      code-review DET phase, using real ast-grep/ctags grounding (added-definition extraction,
      NOT imports-only), shipped advisory/permissive first.
- [ ] MUST-FIRE (new public def, no test) / MUST-NOT-FIRE (def with a correlated test) /
      ABSTAIN (docs-only, unparseable, no backend) unit tests.
- [ ] Proving command: `.venv/bin/pytest tests/unit/test_new_behavior_detector.py -v`.
